text:
Market today: The market mood is cautious, driven by Fed policy uncertainty for 2026 and ongoing U.S.-Iran geopolitical tensions. Concerns are also rising about the boom in risky leveraged ETFs, which amplify market moves and decay. This environment can make your concentrated tech/chip portfolio, especially leveraged positions, particularly vulnerable.

Your holdings: SOXL is down -22.45% and flagged SELL; SPAL is down -19.96% and new. Both are leveraged ETFs, which decay and are extra risky on margin. PTNM is a tiny spec down -15.25% with limited data, also a concern. Remember, margin amplifies all drops.

Watch: Keep an eye on any new Fed commentary regarding 2026 rate hike probabilities and developments in U.S.-Iran relations. Also, closely monitor your leveraged ETF positions for increased volatility.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged positions given market uncertainty and their inherent decay.
plays: SOXL is a leveraged SELL at -22.45%. Consider trimming or respecting the 200 stop to reduce exposure to this decaying asset.
SPAL is a new leveraged position down -19.96%. Watch its 21.38 stop closely to manage risk on this fast-moving ETF.
No new buys today - hold and watch. No compelling catalyst from headlines aligns with scanner setups for new positions.
